# ReactBox
> ReactBox is a type-safe polymorphic React box component with flexible rendering and smart defaults. It provides a powerful foundation for building reusable UI components with TypeScript support, automatic prop forwarding, and component composition patterns.

**Important notes for agents:**
- ReactBox is a lightweight polymorphic component that can render as any HTML element
- Fully typed with TypeScript and supports the `as` prop for polymorphic rendering
- Zero runtime overhead - just a thin wrapper around React's forwardRef
- Works seamlessly with Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, and any styling solution
- No external dependencies beyond React and React DOM

## Documentation
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/afeiship/react-box): Source code and issues
- [NPM Package](https://www.npmjs.org/package/@jswork/react-box): Package information and versions
- [Preview/Demo](https://afeiship.github.io/react-box/): Live preview and examples

## Key Features
- **Type-Safe Polymorphic Component**: Render any HTML element with full TypeScript support
- **Smart Prop Forwarding**: Automatically forwards props to the underlying element
- **ForwardRef Support**: Full ref forwarding for DOM access and integration
- **Zero Runtime Cost**: Minimal abstraction layer with no performance penalty
- **Framework Agnostic**: Works with Next.js, Remix, Vite, and any React setup
- **Tailwind CSS Friendly**: Designed to work perfectly with utility-first CSS

## Installation

### Using npm
```bash
npm install @jswork/react-box
```

### Using yarn
```bash
yarn add @jswork/react-box
```

### Using pnpm
```bash
pnpm add @jswork/react-box
```

## Usage

### Basic Usage
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

function App() {
  return (
    <ReactBox className="p-4 bg-white rounded shadow">
      Hello, ReactBox!
    </ReactBox>
  );
}
```

### Polymorphic Usage
Render different HTML elements using the `as` prop:

```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

// Render as a button
<ReactBox as="button" className="btn btn-primary">
  Click me
</ReactBox>

// Render as a link
<ReactBox as="a" href="/about" className="nav-link">
  About
</ReactBox>

// Render as a section
<ReactBox as="section" className="hero-section">
  Hero Content
</ReactBox>

// Render as a heading
<ReactBox as="h1" className="text-3xl font-bold">
  Title
</ReactBox>
```

### Type-Safe Component Composition
```tsx
import { ReactBox, type Props } from '@jswork/react-box';

type FlexProps = Props<'div'> & {
  direction?: 'row' | 'column';
  align?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end';
  justify?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'between';
};

function Flex({ direction = 'row', align = 'start', justify = 'start', className, ...props }: FlexProps) {
  const classes = [
    'flex',
    direction === 'row' ? 'flex-row' : 'flex-col',
    `align-${align}`,
    `justify-${justify}`,
    className
  ].filter(Boolean).join(' ');

  return <ReactBox className={classes} {...props} />;
}
```

### Styling with Tailwind CSS
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

function StyledComponents() {
  return (
    <>
      {/* Container with spacing */}
      <ReactBox className="container mx-auto px-4">
        Content
      </ReactBox>

      {/* Flex layout */}
      <ReactBox className="flex items-center justify-between gap-4">
        <div>Left</div>
        <div>Right</div>
      </ReactBox>

      {/* Grid layout */}
      <ReactBox className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-6">
        <div>Item 1</div>
        <div>Item 2</div>
        <div>Item 3</div>
      </ReactBox>
    </>
  );
}
```

## API Reference

### ReactBox
The main polymorphic box component.

#### Type Definition
```typescript
interface PolymorphicProps<C extends ElementType> {
  as?: C;
  children?: React.ReactNode;
}

type Props<C extends ElementType> = PolymorphicProps<C> &
  Omit<React.ComponentProps<C>, keyof PolymorphicProps<C>>;

type ReactBoxComponent = <C extends ElementType = 'div'>(
  props: Props<C> & { ref?: React.Ref<React.ElementRef<C>> }
) => React.JSX.Element;
```

#### Props
- `as?: ElementType` - The HTML element or React component to render (default: `'div'`)
- `children?: React.ReactNode` - Child elements
- `className?: string` - CSS class names (inherited from element props)
- `ref?: Ref<React.ElementRef<C>>` - React ref forwarded to the underlying element
- All other HTML attributes of the specified element type are supported

## TypeScript Support

ReactBox is built with TypeScript from the ground up and provides excellent type inference:

```typescript
import { ReactBox, type Props } from '@jswork/react-box';

// Type-safe props for button element
type ButtonProps = Props<'button'> & {
  variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary';
};

function Button({ variant = 'primary', className, ...props }: ButtonProps) {
  const variantClass = variant === 'primary'
    ? 'bg-blue-500 hover:bg-blue-600'
    : 'bg-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-600';

  return (
    <ReactBox
      as="button"
      className={`px-4 py-2 rounded ${variantClass} ${className || ''}`}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}
```

## Usage Notes for Agents

- **Default Element**: ReactBox renders as a `div` by default, but this can be changed with the `as` prop
- **Type Safety**: When using the `as` prop, TypeScript will infer the correct props for that element
- **Ref Forwarding**: Refs are automatically forwarded to the underlying element
- **Styling**: ReactBox is styling-agnostic and works with any CSS solution
- **Props Merging**: All props are forwarded to the underlying element, including className, style, etc.
- **Type Imports**: Use `type Props` to get the correctly typed props for a specific element type

## Examples

### Create a Layout System
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

function Layout() {
  return (
    <ReactBox className="container mx-auto px-4">
      <ReactBox className="flex flex-row gap-4">
        <ReactBox className="flex-1">Column 1</ReactBox>
        <ReactBox className="flex-1">Column 2</ReactBox>
        <ReactBox className="flex-1">Column 3</ReactBox>
      </ReactBox>
    </ReactBox>
  );
}
```

### Create a Design System
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

function Article() {
  return (
    <ReactBox className="article max-w-2xl mx-auto py-8">
      <ReactBox as="h2" className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">
        Article Title
      </ReactBox>
      <ReactBox as="p" className="text-gray-700 leading-relaxed">
        Article content goes here...
      </ReactBox>
    </ReactBox>
  );
}
```

### Interactive Components
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';
import { useState } from 'react';

function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  return (
    <ReactBox className="flex gap-4 items-center">
      <ReactBox as="button" onClick={() => setCount(c => c - 1)} className="px-4 py-2 bg-gray-200 rounded">
        -
      </ReactBox>
      <ReactBox as="span" className="text-xl font-bold">
        {count}
      </ReactBox>
      <ReactBox as="button" onClick={() => setCount(c => c + 1)} className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-500 text-white rounded">
        +
      </ReactBox>
    </ReactBox>
  );
}
```

- **Source**: [packages/lib/src/index.tsx](https://github.com/afeiship/react-box/blob/main/packages/lib/src/index.tsx)
- **Type Definitions**: [packages/lib/dist/main.d.ts](https://github.com/afeiship/react-box/blob/main/packages/lib/dist/main.d.ts)
- **Built with**: TypeScript, React, tsup
